column1: Mirrors the "Test Scripts Master Copy" tab in the AI Learning App - Task Tracker Google Sheet, so the script can be referenced/diffed alongside code changes. The Google Sheet is the source of truth for actually running a test pass (dropdowns, screenshot links, live edits) — update this file to match it after any edit there.

column2: Columns A, B, and C show the Area, Scenario, and Step. A scenario is one thing we're testing, and some scenarios take a few steps to finish. Do the steps in order (Step 1, Step 2, and so on) before moving to the next scenario.
1. Do the Tester Action in column D.
2. Compare what happens to the Expected Result in column E.
3. In column F, use the dropdown to mark Pass, Partial Success, or Blocked. (Partial Success = it worked but something was off; Blocked = something stopped you from finishing the step at all.)

column3: jot a quick note about what you saw.

column2: 5. If you marked Partial Success or Blocked: a screenshot is required so we can see the issue. Paste the link in column H. (Upload the image to your Google Drive, click Share, set it so anyone with the link can view, then copy that link here.)
6. Also in column I, pick a Severity: High (broken or blocks you), Med (works but wrong or confusing), or Low (cosmetic). If you select High or Med, please include notes in column J.

column3: move on to the next row.

column4: Tester Action
column5: Expected Result
column8: Link to Screenshot for Result Fail/Blocked

column4: Open https://learning-agent-pearl.vercel.app in Chrome on a laptop. Note your browser and OS in the Notes column.
column5: App loads without errors; you land on the welcome / identity screen.

column4: On a fresh browser (or an incognito window), open the app.
column5: You're redirected to the sign-in page with a Sign in with Okta button (Okta SSO is now live).

column4: Click Sign in with Okta and log in with your real Housecall Pro account.
column5: You're redirected to housecallpro.okta.com, log in, and land back in the app signed in; your name and email are recognized on later screens.

column4: If you have access to a non housecallpro.com account, try signing in with that instead.
column5: You see an access denied message rather than being let into the app.

column4: Proceed through onboarding to the profile step. Your name, role, and team should already be pre-filled from your Housecall Pro record — review the confirmation card. Correct your name if it's wrong.
column5: Name/role/team are pre-populated correctly from your HCP record (this is expected, not a bug); the name field is editable and any correction carries through. If the prefill is blank or wrong, that's a High-severity flag since it feeds your learning path.

column4: Confirm or change your role/team. If yours isn't listed, choose "Something else not listed here."
column5: Selection is saved; the app says it will tailor your learning path to your team.

column4: On the tool step, set your Primary tool (e.g. Gemini, ChatGPT, Copilot).
column5: Tool is saved as your primary tool.

column4: Add a second tool using 'Add another tool (e.g. Perplexity)'.
column5: Second tool is added and shown in your tool list.

column4: On the goals step, select ONE OR MORE goals you want to learn. This is now a checkbox multi-select, not a single choice.
column5: All selected goals are accepted and saved; you are routed into the app (home or a tailored lesson/path). Note: selecting goals doesn't award XP yet, that's expected.

column4: Later, open My Role (name menu, top right) and check how your goals display.
column5: Goals show as a list, e.g. 'Goals: A, B, C'.

column4: After finishing profile/goal setup, the app should drop you straight into a required, full-screen Calibration before you reach Home. Work through the questions (skill scenarios + the Personal/Team/Org AI Impact questions — one combined flow).
column5: Calibration launches automatically as a full-screen gate you can't skip or dismiss; questions load one at a time; you cannot reach Home until it's done.

column4: Complete the calibration all the way to the end.
column5: You see a "Calibrated" / results state, your skill levels and impact scores save, and you're released into the app. Re-entering later does not force calibration again.

column4: Navigate to Home using BOTH the persistent link in the header (top of any page) and the sidebar.
column5: Both routes land you on Home with a personalized greeting (Good morning/afternoon/evening + your name) and your tier/level badge.

column4: On Home, find the "Find AI for your work" card (under "Find something to learn") and click its "Discover what AI can do for you" button.
column5: You're taken to Discovery
column6: showing AI use cases relevant to your role/tasks.

column4: On Home, find the 'Today's Pick' card and click it.
column5: A relevant lesson/activity opens that matches your role/goals.

column4: After it opens
column5: navigate back to Home using the header link.
column6: You land back on Home cleanly, with no errors or getting stuck mid-lesson.

column4: On Home, use one of the "Other ways to learn" tiles (e.g. start a lesson, open a game).
column5: The action routes you to the correct destination.

column4: Once there
column5: navigate back to Home to confirm it's reachable again.
column6: You return to Home without errors.

column4: On Home, locate the streak card and XP/stats pills.
column5: Streak count and XP totals display and reflect your activity (not blank/zero after completing something).

column4: If they're clickable, tap into them, then navigate back to Home.
column5: You return to Home and the same numbers are still shown correctly.

column4: On Home, view the leaderboard widget, then click into it if it's clickable.
column5: Leaderboard renders with names/scores, your position is visible.

column4: If you've earned XP earlier in this session (a lesson, game, or challenge), check whether your position here reflects it.
column5: Your position moves up (or your total updates) to match the XP you've earned, rather than showing a stale ranking.

column4: Click the single bell icon in the header and review its contents.
column5: One dropdown shows a combined newest-first feed — notifications (XP earned, badges, level-ups, streaks) and any unfinished/paused lessons. Badge count = unread notifications + paused lessons.

column4: Click a paused lesson's resume (Play) button, and/or "View all unfinished lessons" at the bottom.
column5: Resume drops you back into that lesson where you left off; "View all unfinished lessons" opens the full paused-lessons list.

column4: Open Discovery (sidebar: Learn > Discovery).
column5: A discovery view loads with AI use cases relevant to your role.

column4: Pick a use case that interests you and open it.
column5: Detail/lesson opens and is relevant; no broken content.

column4: Open Library (sidebar: Learn > Library).
column5: Categories display as collapsible groups with colored pills.

column4: Expand and collapse several categories.
column5: Categories expand/collapse smoothly and show use cases inside.

column4: From Library, open a specific use case.
column5: Use case content loads correctly and is readable.

column4: Go to Lesson (or generate from a topic). Enter a specific topic (e.g. 'Writing follow-up emails to customers with AI').
column5: A lesson is generated on that exact topic with no clarify prompt.

column4: Read through the generated lesson slides.
column5: Content is coherent, on-topic, and free of obvious errors or placeholder text.

column4: Generate a lesson from a vague topic (e.g. 'AI' or 'help me').
column5: The app asks a clarifying question instead of generating a generic lesson.

column4: Answer the clarifying prompt with specifics.
column5: A focused lesson is then generated matching your clarification.

column4: In a lesson with narration, press Play on the audio.
column5: Narration plays clearly and is in sync with the current slide.

column4: Advance to the next slide while audio plays.
column5: Audio transitions appropriately to the new slide; no overlap of two narrations.

column4: Start a lesson, then leave it midway (navigate away).
column5: The lesson is saved as paused (paused-lessons bell shows a count).

column4: Open the paused-lessons bell and resume the lesson.
column5: You return to the same lesson at (or near) where you left off.

column4: Inside a lesson, use the refine/regenerate option to make it simpler or more specific.
column5: Lesson regenerates reflecting your refinement; old content is replaced.

column4: Complete a lesson that ends with a quiz/interactive activity.
column5: Quiz works (can select answers

column4: Finish the lesson using the Finish button (e.g. "Finish Deep Dive" or "Finish & earn XP").
column5: A completion state shows and you receive the XP shown on the Finish button (XP toast/popup appears).

column4: Check the Home XP pill or Progress > Leaderboard to confirm the XP you just earned actually landed.
column5: Your total XP increased by the amount shown on the Finish button, and your leaderboard position reflects it.

column4: Open a lesson that includes video and play it.
column5: Video loads and plays with working controls; no broken embed.

column4: Start a lesson and choose the Project Quest format (note where you found it / how you started it).
column5: A multi-step
column6: project-based quest loads and is completable; progress is tracked.

column4: Open Just Chat (sidebar: Learn > Just Chat) and ask a general AI question.
column5: You get a helpful coaching response; it is distinct from the lesson-generation flow.

column4: Check the footer of Just Chat for pinned Slack help channels.
column5: Footer shows links to both HCP Skill Shop Help and HCP MX Skill Shop Help channels.

column4: Open Prompts (sidebar: Learn > Prompts) and browse prompts.
column5: Prompts list loads, organized/labeled clearly.

column4: Copy a prompt using its copy control.
column5: Prompt copies to clipboard and a confirmation appears.

column4: Open Today's Pick (sidebar: Learn > Today's Pick).
column5: A daily pick/activity loads for today's date.

column4: Complete today's activity.
column5: Completion is recorded and XP is awarded (once daily).

column4: Confirm the XP landed in your total and check whether your leaderboard position updated.
column5: Your XP total went up by the right amount, and your position on the leaderboard reflects it.

column4: In the sidebar under "HCP Skill Shop," click each link (AI Resources Home, Level 1, Level 2, Level 3).
column5: Each opens the correct Docebo course/page in a new tab; no broken links.

column4: Open Games (sidebar: Learn > Games) and pick a game (Prompt Battle / Hallucination Hunt / Speed Round / AI or Human?).
column5: Game opens to a start screen with a minimized 'How to play' you can expand.

column4: Start and play a full round of the chosen game.
column5: Game is playable end-to-end; a score is shown at the end.

column4: Observe XP after finishing.
column5: XP is awarded based on difficulty times score; XP toast/popup appears.

column4: Check the Home leaderboard widget or Progress > Leaderboard to confirm your position reflects the new XP.
column5: You see your position move up (or your total update) rather than showing a stale ranking.

column4: Replay the same game again on the same day.
column5: You can replay, but no additional XP is granted for the repeat (per content-day rule).

column4: Check the leaderboard after playing.
column5: Your game performance is reflected in your XP/standing.

column4: In a lesson, use the 'open tool' / LLM window callout link for your preferred tool.
column5: Your preferred AI tool opens in a new tab (e.g. Gemini), with guidance to use it.

column4: Go to My AI Tools (name menu) and change your preferred AI tool.
column5: Selection saves and subsequent 'open tool' links point to the new tool.

column4: Open Achievements (sidebar: Your Progress > Achievements).
column5: Earned and locked achievements display; earned ones reflect your activity.

column4: Open Knowledge Heatmap (sidebar: Your Progress > Knowledge Heatmap).
column5: Heatmap renders with skill areas with strength shading.

column4: Open Leaderboard (sidebar: Your Progress > Leaderboard).
column5: Full leaderboard loads with rankings; your row is identifiable and reflects the XP you've earned this session, not a stale ranking.

column4: Open My Calibration from the name menu (top right), then open My Impact from the name menu.
column5: My Calibration shows your calibration summary + history with a "Recalibrate" button. My Impact shows your Personal/Team/Org impact scores as a read-only view (the questions are answered inside calibration, not here).

column4: From My Calibration use "Recalibrate" (or from My Impact use "Go to calibration") and update your answers.
column5: You can re-run the combined calibration + impact flow; updated scores save and feed the Manager Dashboard — flag clearly if this breaks.

column4: Open My Profile from the name menu (top right).
column5: Your profile page loads with your details (name, avatar, role/team); info is accurate and editable where expected.

column4: Open My Role from the name menu (top right).
column5: The page shows your current department/sub-team and goals.

column4: Change your department/sub-team or goals and choose Apply now.
column5: Change applies immediately with confirmation shown.

column4: Try "Schedule for a date", then check the History/Revert option.
column5: Scheduled change and revert option both work as expected.

column4: Open My Tasks from the name menu (top right).
column5: Your task list loads (or shows an empty state).

column4: Add a new task (there's no cap on how many you can add).
column5: Task saves and appears in the list.

column4: Open Projects (name menu) and describe a real work project.
column5: Project saves; the app notes lessons will be tailored around it.

column4: Use the back arrow from the Projects page.
column5: The back arrow returns you to the previous page (falling back to Home only if there's no history). That's expected

column4: Toggle the theme between light and dark.
column5: Entire UI switches cleanly; text stays readable, no broken/white patches.

column4: Start the Tour (sidebar: Settings > Tour).
column5: An interactive tour begins on Home and spotlights real

column4: Follow the tour through its Home highlights (Find AI, quick tiles, Today's Pick, Level & XP, leaderboard, skills, AI News), then the menu, profile dropdown, and Help widget.
column5: Each element is spotlighted in order; the tour is Home-focused, then points out the sidebar menu, profile dropdown, and Help widget.

column4: Complete or exit the tour.
column5: Tour ends gracefully and returns you to normal use.

column4: Open the Help widget, type a message but don't send it, navigate to a different page and come back.
column5: Your typed text and conversation are still there; it now persists across navigation, not just while the widget stays open.

column4: Look for a Log Out option in the name menu (top right).
column5: A Log Out option is present (Okta SSO is now live, so this should always be there).

column4: Log out, then sign back in through Okta with the same account.
column5: You're signed out and returned to the sign-in page, then signing back in restores your profile, XP, and progress intact (not reset, no fresh welcome bonus re-granted).

column4: Open Settings > Voice and change/preview the narration voice.
column5: The selection saves; narrated playback uses the chosen voice.

column4: Click your name/avatar in the top right on a few different pages (e.g. Home, a Lesson, Library).
column5: The menu appears consistently on every page and contains: My Profile, My Role, My Tasks, My Projects, My AI Tools, My Calibration, My Impact, and Log out. (No "Switch user".)

column4: Open Team Dashboard (sidebar: Manager > Team Dashboard).
column5: Dashboard loads showing your team's learning activity/metrics.

column4: Review the team metrics for accuracy/sensibility.
column5: Numbers look reasonable (no obviously broken/empty/NaN values).

column4: Open Reporting (sidebar: Manager > Reporting).
column5: Reporting view loads with charts/tables that render correctly.

column4: Open the app on a phone (or narrow the browser window).
column5: Layout adapts; navigation is reachable; nothing is cut off or overlapping.

column4: Throughout testing, note any error messages, broken images, slow loads (>5s), or confusing wording.
column5: Capture each issue with the page, what you did, and what you expected in Notes.

column4: After finishing
column5: rate how intuitive the app was (1-5) and note the single most confusing thing.
column6: Subjective feedback recorded for the team.
